asset_type: wide TaraHome technical blog cover for a non-product Matter troubleshooting article
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: wide TaraHome technical blog cover for a non-product Matter troubleshooting article
Primary request: a clean editorial photograph that suggests one smart-home device being controlled through several ordinary household paths
Scene/backdrop: warm, lived-in modern living room with natural oak, linen upholstery, off-white plaster, one restrained dark-green ceramic accent, and subtle real-home texture
Subject: one simple glowing table lamp as the clear focal device; a homeowner naturally reaching for an ordinary wall switch while a smartphone with a completely dark unreadable screen rests on the oak side table and a small unbranded smart speaker sits quietly farther back; the objects belong naturally in the room and are not arranged like a diagram
Style/medium: photorealistic residential editorial photography, candid and believable, not a render
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, eye-level, balanced for both full article hero and center article-card crop, lamp and wall-switch interaction remain recognizable at small size, gentle background depth of field
Lighting/mood: soft late-afternoon window light mixed with the warm lamp, calm, practical, trustworthy, true-to-life color
Color palette: warm wood and cream with restrained Tara dark green accents
Materials/textures: visible oak grain, woven linen, matte ceramic, natural skin and fabric texture
Constraints: no readable screen content, no text, no logo, no watermark, no embedded headline or title poster, no exact branded hardware, no fake chart or graph, no decorative routing/network/path lines, no connection trails, no translucent panels, no inset frames, no floating UI cards, no progress bars
Avoid: generic AI-looking smart-home diagram, sci-fi interface, staged luxury showroom, excessive beige, duplicated objects, distorted hands, glossy CGI surfaces
